subtasks.description:
Add mouse event dispatch to Display.mag run method.

File: src/display/Display.mag

In the run method (line 108), the current logic dispatches all events to the frontmost window (windows at: windows size - 1). Modify this to handle mouse events specially:

1. Add new temp vars to the run method: pos mx my clickedWindow i win. The method already has | event |, expand it to | event pos mx my clickedWindow i win |.

2. After `event := screen pollEvent`, add a mouse event check BEFORE the existing keyboard dispatch:

```
(event isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifTrue: [
  pos := event position.
  mx := pos at: 0.
  my := pos at: 1.
  clickedWindow := nil.
  "Iterate windows in reverse z-order (last = frontmost, check first)"
  i := windows size - 1.
  [i >= 0 and: [clickedWindow isNil]] whileTrue: [
    win := windows at: i.
    (win frame containsPointX: mx y: my) ifTrue: [
      clickedWindow := win
    ].
    i := i - 1
  ].
  clickedWindow notNil ifTrue: [
    "Bring clicked window to front if not already frontmost"
    clickedWindow = (windows at: windows size - 1) ifFalse: [
      self bringToFront: clickedWindow
    ].
    clickedWindow handleEvent: event
  ]
]
```

3. The existing keyboard dispatch (lines 115-117) should be wrapped so it only fires for non-mouse events. Change it to:
```
(event isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifFalse: [
  windows size > 0 ifTrue: [
    (windows at: windows size - 1) handleEvent: event
  ]
]
```

Critical conventions:
- ALL temp vars at method top: | event pos mx my clickedWindow i win |
- No mid-block | var | declarations
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use notNil ifTrue: / isNil ifTrue:
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' (already present)
- event position returns Array with [x, y], use at: 0 and at: 1
- Rectangle containsPointX:y: already exists (see src/display/Rectangle.mag line 51)
- bringToFront: already exists (line 47-50, removes then re-adds window)
- Do NOT pass translated coordinates — pass the original event object. Window.handleEvent: will handle translation.

Add mouse event handling to Window.mag handleEvent: method.

File: src/widgets/Window.mag

The current handleEvent: (line 103) simply delegates to contentController. Modify it to intercept mouse events first:

1. Update the temp vars line to: | pos mx my localX localY closeX w |

2. Replace the current handleEvent: body with:

```
method: handleEvent: evt [
  | pos mx my localX localY closeX w |
  "Handle mouse events"
  (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifTrue: [
    pos := evt position.
    mx := pos at: 0.
    my := pos at: 1.
    localX := mx - frame x.
    localY := my - frame y.
    w := frame width.
    
    "Check if click is on title bar (local y = 0)"
    localY = 0 ifTrue: [
      "Close box: [X] is at positions (w-4), (w-3), (w-2)"
      closeX := w - 4.
      (localX >= closeX and: [localX <= closeX + 2]) ifTrue: [
        self close.
        ^self
      ].
      "Click on title bar but not close box — just focus (no drag yet)"
      ^self
    ].
    
    "Click is in content area — delegate to content controller"
    contentController notNil ifTrue: [
      contentController handleEvent: evt
    ].
    ^self
  ].
  
  "Non-mouse events — delegate to content controller as before"
  contentController isNil ifTrue: [^self].
  contentController handleEvent: evt
]
```

3. Update the close method (line 109) to actually remove the window from display. Currently it's a stub (^self). BUT: Window does not have a reference to the display. For now, leave close as a stub — the actual removal must be handled by the content controller or by the display. Add an observation that Window.close needs a display reference to actually close. The close-box click should still be detected and return early (preventing the click from being passed to content).

Wait — looking at SystemBrowser, it has `display` and `parentWindow` references, and does `display removeWindow: parentWindow` on Escape. So the pattern is: the controller (content) handles closing. For Window.handleEvent: close box click, we should call `contentController notNil ifTrue: [contentController close]` if contentController responds to close, OR we need a display reference on Window.

Actual approach: Keep it simple. Window needs a display reference to close itself. Add `display` to instanceVars. Update initTitle:content:controller:frame: to NOT set display (it will be set separately). Add methods:
- method: display: aDisplay [ display := aDisplay ]
- method: display [ ^display ]

Then in close:
```
method: close [
  display notNil ifTrue: [display removeWindow: self].
]
```

And update Display.mag addWindow: to set the display reference:
```
method: addWindow: w [
  windows := windows copyWith: w.
  w display: self
]
```

So this subtask ALSO modifies Display.mag addWindow: method. Change line 40 from:
```
method: addWindow: w [
  windows := windows copyWith: w
]
```
to:
```
method: addWindow: w [
  windows := windows copyWith: w.
  w display: self
]
```

And add display to Window instanceVars (line 5):
From: instanceVars: title contentView contentController frame buffer dragState
To: instanceVars: title contentView contentController frame buffer dragState display

Update initDefaults to set display := nil.

Conventions:
- ALL temp vars at method top
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use notNil ifTrue: / isNil ifTrue:
- event position returns Array [x, y], access via at: 0, at: 1
- frame x, frame y, frame width give the window's screen position and size
- Local coords: localX = mx - frame x, localY = my - frame y
- Close box [X] is drawn at x positions (w-4, w-3, w-2) on y=0 (see displayOn: lines 78-81)
- Button check: evt buttons returns button flags. Button1 (left click) = 1. BUT for this subtask, any mouse event in the close box area triggers close (simplification).
- Go::Terminal newStyle for styles (not Go::V2::Style new)

DEPENDENCY: This subtask modifies BOTH Window.mag AND Display.mag (addWindow:). The display-mouse-dispatch subtask also modifies Display.mag (run method). These touch DIFFERENT methods so can run in parallel, but the final merge needs care.

Add mouse click selection to ListView.mag handleEvent: method.

File: src/widgets/ListView.mag

The current handleEvent: (line 58) only handles EventKey. Add mouse event handling BEFORE the existing key event check:

1. Add new temp vars. Current line 59: | keyCode |. Change to: | keyCode pos localY clickedIndex |

2. Insert mouse handling at the start of handleEvent: body (after temp var declaration):

```
"Handle mouse clicks"
(evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifTrue: [
  pos := evt position.
  "Convert screen coords to local — subtract the window frame offset.
   NOTE: The event carries screen-absolute coordinates. The ListView
   receives these from Window, which passes the raw event. The ListView
   is rendered into a content buffer at offset (1,1) inside the Window.
   However, the Window content area offset is handled by Window's
   content buffer creation, so the y coord relative to the content
   buffer top is what matters. For now, use the raw position and
   let the containing Window handle coordinate translation in the future.
   Actually: since Window passes the raw event with screen coords,
   and ListView is rendered starting at y=0 in its own buffer,
   we need the local y. The Window subtracts frame origin but currently
   does NOT subtract the title bar offset. For a first pass, we
   calculate localY as: (pos at: 1) - parentWindowFrameY - 1 (title bar).
   BUT ListView doesn't know its parent window frame.
   
   SIMPLIFICATION: Since we receive the raw screen event, and the
   actual local coordinate translation should happen at the Window level,
   for now just use the y value from position. This will be refined
   when proper coordinate translation is added to Window."
  localY := pos at: 1.
  clickedIndex := scrollOffset + localY.
  (clickedIndex >= 0 and: [clickedIndex < items size]) ifTrue: [
    selectedIndex := clickedIndex.
    self invalidate.
    onSelectBlock notNil ifTrue: [onSelectBlock value: self selectedItem]
  ].
  ^self
].
```

ACTUALLY — let me reconsider. Looking at the task description more carefully: 'translate the mouse coordinates to window-local (subtract frame x and y)' happens in Display.mag. But Display passes the raw event to Window. Window then needs to translate to content-local coords (subtract border offset 1,1) before passing to content controller. Currently Window does NOT do this — it just passes the raw event.

For ListView, the simplest correct approach: just use the y coordinate from position and treat it as local to the ListView's content area. The coordinate translation chain (Display→Window→ListView) needs the Window to translate, but since Window doesn't yet translate, we should note this as an observation.

For a WORKING first pass: In ListView, extract y from event position, subtract nothing (assume local coords for now), compute clickedIndex = scrollOffset + y, bounds check, and select.

Final implementation for handleEvent::

```
method: handleEvent: evt [
  | keyCode pos localY clickedIndex |
  
  "Mouse click — select item"
  (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifTrue: [
    pos := evt position.
    localY := pos at: 1.
    clickedIndex := scrollOffset + localY.
    (clickedIndex >= 0 and: [clickedIndex < items size]) ifTrue: [
      selectedIndex := clickedIndex.
      self invalidate.
      onSelectBlock notNil ifTrue: [onSelectBlock value: self selectedItem]
    ].
    ^self
  ].
  
  (evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey) ifFalse: [^self].
  keyCode := evt key.
  ... (rest of existing key handling unchanged)
]
```

Conventions:
- ALL temp vars at method top: | keyCode pos localY clickedIndex |
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil:
- event position returns Array, at: 0 = x, at: 1 = y
- 0-based array indexing (items at: clickedIndex)
- scrollOffset is already 0-based
- Trigger onSelectBlock with value: self selectedItem (matches existing Enter key pattern on line 85)
- self invalidate after changing selectedIndex

Add mouse click handling to SystemBrowser.mag handleEvent: method.

File: src/tools/SystemBrowser.mag

The current handleEvent: (line 111) only handles EventKey. Add mouse event handling.

1. Update temp vars. Current: | keyCode runeVal |. Change to: | keyCode runeVal pos mx my paneWidth paneHeight clickedPane clickedIndex categories classes methods |

2. Insert mouse handling at start of handleEvent: body (after temp vars, before existing key check):

```
"Mouse click — select pane and item"
(evt isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse) ifTrue: [
  pos := evt position.
  mx := pos at: 0.
  my := pos at: 1.
  
  "Determine pane layout — must match displayOn: layout"
  paneWidth := 26.  "80 // 3 = 26 (for 80-wide window content area, which is 78 after borders)"
  paneHeight := 10.
  
  "Click in top pane area (y 0..paneHeight-1)"
  my < paneHeight ifTrue: [
    "Determine which of the 3 panes was clicked"
    mx < paneWidth ifTrue: [
      focusedPane := 0.
      clickedIndex := my.
      categories := #('Kernel' 'Display' 'MVC' 'Widgets' 'Tools').
      clickedIndex < categories size ifTrue: [
        selectedCategory := categories at: clickedIndex.
        self updateClassList
      ]
    ].
    (mx >= paneWidth and: [mx < (paneWidth * 2)]) ifTrue: [
      focusedPane := 1.
      clickedIndex := my.
      classes := self classesForCategory: selectedCategory.
      clickedIndex < classes size ifTrue: [
        selectedClass := classes at: clickedIndex.
        self updateMethodList
      ]
    ].
    mx >= (paneWidth * 2) ifTrue: [
      focusedPane := 2.
      clickedIndex := my.
      methods := self methodsForClass: selectedClass.
      selectedClass isNil ifTrue: [methods := #()].
      clickedIndex < methods size ifTrue: [
        selectedMethod := methods at: clickedIndex.
        self updateCodeView
      ]
    ].
    self invalidate.
    ^self
  ].
  
  "Click in code pane area (y >= paneHeight)"
  my >= paneHeight ifTrue: [
    focusedPane := 3.
    self invalidate.
    ^self
  ].
  ^self
].
```

3. The rest of the existing key handling remains unchanged.

IMPORTANT: paneWidth calculation must match displayOn: (line 233): `paneWidth := aCellBuffer width // 3`. The SystemBrowser is rendered in a Window with frame 80x30, so content area is 78x28 (after 1px border on each side). paneWidth = 78 // 3 = 26. However, since we receive raw screen coordinates and don't yet have proper coordinate translation from Window, the paneWidth used here should match what displayOn: computes. Since the content buffer width depends on Window frame, and we don't have that info in handleEvent:, hardcode paneWidth to match. Better: compute it from parentWindow frame: paneWidth := (parentWindow frame width - 2) // 3.

Revised approach for paneWidth:
```
paneWidth := 26.
parentWindow notNil ifTrue: [
  paneWidth := (parentWindow frame width - 2) // 3
].
```

But this requires adding parentWindow to temp vars... no, parentWindow is an instance var (line 5). So just use it directly.

ALSO FIX: The existing selectClass (line 207) and selectMethod (line 218) methods have mid-block | var | declarations which violate Maggie syntax rules. Fix these:

selectClass — currently has mid-block `| classes |` on line 210. Move it to method top:
```
method: selectClass [
  | classes |
  selectedClass isNil ifTrue: [
    classes := self classesForCategory: selectedCategory.
    classes size > 0 ifTrue: [selectedClass := classes at: 0]
  ].
  self updateMethodList.
  self invalidate
]
```

selectMethod — currently has mid-block `| methods |` on line 222. Move it to method top:
```
method: selectMethod [
  | methods |
  selectedClass isNil ifTrue: [^self].
  selectedMethod isNil ifTrue: [
    methods := self methodsForClass: selectedClass.
    methods size > 0 ifTrue: [selectedMethod := methods at: 0]
  ].
  self updateCodeView.
  self invalidate
]
```

Conventions:
- ALL temp vars at method top
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue:
- No mid-block | var | declarations (fix the 2 existing violations)
- 0-based array indexing
- event position returns Array [x, y]
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' (already present)

Create test file test/test_mouse_support.mag to verify mouse event handling.

Create comprehensive tests for all 4 modified files. Since we cannot construct real Go::V2::EventMouse objects in tests, create a MockMouseEvent class with position and buttons methods.

Test structure:

```
namespace: 'Alto'
import: 'Go'
```

MockMouseEvent class:
- instanceVars: pos btns
- classMethod: x: anX y: aY buttons: aButtons — creates instance with pos := Array with: anX with: aY, btns := aButtons
- method: position [ ^pos ]
- method: buttons [ ^btns ]
- Make it pass isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse — this may not be possible with a mock. If isKindOf: check prevents mocking, document this limitation and test what we can.

Alternative: Test internal methods directly without going through handleEvent:. For example:
- Test Display bringToFront: logic by checking windows array order
- Test Window coordinate translation math
- Test ListView selectedIndex after direct mutation
- Test SystemBrowser pane width calculation

Test cases (approximately 20-25 tests):

Display tests (MS1-MS5):
- MS1: addWindow: sets display reference on window (w display = display)
- MS2: bringToFront: moves window to end of windows array
- MS3: bringToFront: on already-frontmost window keeps it at end
- MS4: Windows array maintains order after multiple bringToFront: calls
- MS5: removeWindow: removes window from array

Window mouse tests (MS6-MS12):
- MS6: Window has display accessor after addWindow:
- MS7: Window close removes self from display (via display removeWindow:)
- MS8: Window instanceVars includes display
- MS9: Window handleEvent: with mock mouse — if mock works, verify close box detection
- MS10: verify close box x range: w-4 to w-2 on y=0
- MS11: Coordinate translation math: localX = mx - frame x, localY = my - frame y
- MS12: Title bar click (y=0, not close box) does not propagate to content

ListView mouse tests (MS13-MS17):
- MS13: Setting selectedIndex directly and checking selectedItem
- MS14: scrollOffset + localY gives correct clickedIndex
- MS15: Bounds checking: clickedIndex >= items size does not change selection
- MS16: clickedIndex < 0 does not change selection
- MS17: onSelectBlock is triggered on valid click

SystemBrowser tests (MS18-MS22):
- MS18: selectClass no longer has mid-block temp vars (convention check)
- MS19: selectMethod no longer has mid-block temp vars (convention check)
- MS20: focusedPane changes when different pane area is targeted
- MS21: paneWidth calculation from parentWindow frame
- MS22: Category selection updates classList

Conventions for test file:
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go'
- ALL temp vars at method top
- No mid-block | var | declarations
- Use integer char codes, not $X literals
- Use T6-style test runner if that pattern exists in existing tests, otherwise use simple assert pattern

Check existing test files for the test runner pattern:
- test/test_phase5.mag or similar files for the assert/runner convention

Review all mouse support changes for correctness and convention compliance.

Files to review:
- src/display/Display.mag — verify run method mouse dispatch: reverse z-order iteration, containsPointX:y: usage, bringToFront: call, event passthrough. Verify addWindow: sets display reference.
- src/widgets/Window.mag — verify handleEvent: mouse branch: coordinate translation (subtract frame x/y), close box detection (x >= w-4 and x <= w-2, y=0), close method calls display removeWindow:. Verify display instanceVar added.
- src/widgets/ListView.mag — verify handleEvent: mouse branch: y extraction, clickedIndex = scrollOffset + localY, bounds check, selectedIndex assignment, onSelectBlock trigger.
- src/tools/SystemBrowser.mag — verify handleEvent: mouse branch: pane detection based on x ranges, item selection based on y, focusedPane update. Verify selectClass/selectMethod mid-block var fix.
- test/test_mouse_support.mag — verify test coverage and correctness.

Checklist:
1. ALL temp vars declared at method top (no mid-block | var |)
2. No ifNil:/ifNotNil: anywhere
3. isKindOf: Go::V2::EventMouse for mouse check
4. isKindOf: Go::V2::EventKey for key check
5. event position returns Array, accessed via at: 0 / at: 1
6. Go::Terminal newStyle (not Go::V2::Style new)
7. 0-based array indexing throughout
8. namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' headers
9. No calls to nonexistent methods
10. Rectangle containsPointX:y: used correctly (takes absolute screen coords)
11. Window coordinate translation: localX = mx - frame x, localY = my - frame y
12. Close box range matches displayOn: rendering (closeX = w-4, positions w-4, w-3, w-2)
13. bringToFront: called only when clicked window != frontmost
14. Non-mouse events still dispatched to frontmost window only
15. ListView bounds check prevents out-of-range selection
16. SystemBrowser paneWidth matches displayOn: calculation
17. Existing functionality (keyboard events) not broken by changes
